missing: Rules family (entire product group, /rules/ + /ruleset-engine/) — completely absent. Each is a distinct 'stop hand-editing nginx / Lambda@Edge / origin code' replacement: Transform Rules (rewrite request/response headers & URLs — replaces nginx rewrite/headers-more or Lambda@Edge), Cloudflare Snippets (lightweight JS at the edge without a full Worker — replaces tiny Lambda@Edge/VCL snippets), Cloud Connector (route traffic to cloud storage/origins by rule — replaces a reverse-proxy/origin-router box), Redirect Rules / Bulk Redirects & Single Redirects (replaces a redirect-map service, _redirects files, or nginx return-301 maps and bulk redirect spreadsheets), Origin Rules (override host/port/SNI/path to origin — replaces nginx proxy_pass juggling), Configuration Rules (per-path Cloudflare settings — replaces conditional config in your edge), Compression Rules, Managed Transforms, and Custom Errors (branded error pages — replaces custom 5xx error-page hosting). This is the single biggest catalog gap on the developer/CDN side.

Cloudflare Access (Zero Trust Network Access) — folded into the 'Cloudflare One' umbrella but never given its own entry. It is Cloudflare's flagship ZTNA product and the clearest standalone 'replaces' story: replaces Okta/Duo/Azure AD application gating + per-app VPN, an identity-aware reverse proxy (Pomerium/oauth2-proxy/Teleport), and bastion/jump-host SSH access. Cloudflare's own marketing frames it as 'stop hosting your VPN.'

Cloudflare Gateway (Secure Web Gateway) — also buried in the 'Cloudflare One' umbrella. Distinct product: DNS/HTTP/network filtering egress proxy — replaces Cisco Umbrella / Zscaler Internet Access / Netskope SWG, on-prem Squid/pfSense+pi-hole content filtering, and DNS-filtering appliances. Pairs with WARP as the on-ramp.

Cloudflare Browser Isolation (Remote Browser Isolation / RBI) — separate Cloudflare One product with no catalog entry. Replaces Menlo Security / Citrix (Ericom) RBI / Symantec Web Isolation, or a fleet of disposable VDI/Kasm/streamed-Chrome browsers stood up to render risky web content away from the endpoint.

Cloudflare Email Security (the Area 1 acquisition, now under Cloudflare One / cloudflare-one/email-security) — entirely missing. This is INBOUND email threat protection (anti-phishing/BEC/malware), categorically different from the catalog's 'Cloudflare Email Service' (outbound transactional sending) and 'DMARC Management'. Replaces Proofpoint / Mimecast / Abnormal Security / Microsoft Defender for Office 365.

Cloudflare CASB and Cloudflare DLP as standalone entries — the catalog's 'Cloudflare One' line name-drops 'separate CASB and DLP vendors' inside one umbrella, but neither gets a discoverable entry. CASB replaces Netskope/McAfee MVISION/Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (SaaS posture + misconfiguration scanning for Google Workspace/M365/Salesforce/etc.); DLP replaces Symantec/Forcepoint/Microsoft Purview DLP (sensitive-data detection in proxied traffic, with built-in PII/PCI/financial detection profiles).

Digital Experience Monitoring (DEX) — Cloudflare One's endpoint/network telemetry product (synthetic + traceroute + Fleet Status from the WARP client). No catalog entry. Replaces Catchpoint / ThousandEyes endpoint agents / Nexthink for last-mile digital-experience monitoring of remote workers.

Cloudflare Network settings product (/network/) — toggles for HTTP/2, HTTP/3 (QUIC), gRPC, WebSockets, IPv6 compatibility, Onion Routing, and pseudo-IPv4. Has its own llms.txt and is not covered by any catalog entry; closest is the Cache/CDN line. Minor but a real standalone index entry the catalog omits.

Risk Score / UEBA (Cloudflare One Insights) — user-risk behavioral scoring that feeds Zero Trust policy. Appears as its own product in Cloudflare's changelog taxonomy; no catalog entry. Replaces a UEBA add-on (e.g. Microsoft Entra ID Protection, Exabeam-style user behavior analytics).

Workers Builds (Workers CI/CD: git-connected build & deploy for Workers) — under the Workers umbrella in llms.txt, so optional, but it is the direct 'replaces GitHub Actions/CircleCI build-and-deploy pipeline for Workers' analog and is arguably worth its own line given the catalog already breaks out comparably-scoped sub-products elsewhere.
thin: Cloudflare Web3 Gateways [medium] — flagged medium and reads memory-only; should be re-grounded against /web3/ docs (Ethereum + IPFS gateway, and the hostname-based gateway model) to confirm it is gateways-to-networks rather than node-hosting, since the 'replaces running your own IPFS node' framing is only half right — it is read gateway access, not pinning/RPC parity with Infura/Alchemy.

Cloudflare DMARC Management [medium] — re-ground against /dmarc-management/ docs; it is a fairly narrow feature (parses RUA aggregate reports surfaced inside the dashboard via Email Routing), and the catalog should make explicit it does NOT do enforcement/BIMI/inbound protection (that is Email Security), to avoid overstating scope.

Multi-Cloud Networking [medium] — confirm current scope against /multi-cloud-networking/; it evolved from Magic Cloud Networking and the 'replaces AWS TGW + Azure vWAN + GCP NCC' framing should be checked against what it actually automates today (cloud resource discovery + flow/firewall visibility) versus full cross-cloud transit.

Cloudflare Randomness Beacon [medium] — verify drand/League of Entropy framing against /randomness-beacon/; the 'replaces commit-reveal / audited RNG oracle' analogy is plausible but memory-derived and worth one doc pass.

Cloudflare One [high] — marked high but is doing far too much work as a single umbrella: it is the parent SASE platform, and the catalog leans on it to implicitly cover Access, Gateway, Browser Isolation, CASB, DLP, Email Security, and DEX. The umbrella entry itself is fine, but its high confidence masks that the sub-products underneath are uncovered (see missing). Re-scope it as an explicit parent-of rather than a substitute for the individual entries.

Cloudflare Realtime (RealtimeKit + SFU + TURN) [high] — naming churn risk; RealtimeKit is the rebrand of the acquired Dyte product and 'Calls' was the former name of the SFU/TURN piece. Worth one doc pass against /realtime/ to confirm the current sub-component names and that 'Calls' is fully retired, since the catalog should not resurrect a dead name in adjacent entries.

Cloudflare Speed (Observatory) [high] — current index label is just 'Speed'; verify the 'Observatory' sub-name is still the active feature name and that the RUM/Speed Brain/prefetch framing matches current /speed/ docs rather than the older Observatory-only scope.
corrections: The 'Cloudflare One [high]' entry conflates a parent platform with a product substitute and absorbs CASB+DLP into a single line. Per Cloudflare's own taxonomy, Access, Gateway, Browser Isolation, CASB, DLP, Email Security, DEX, and Risk Score are individually-documented products. The 'replaces a VPN concentrator + a legacy SWG + separate CASB and DLP vendors' framing is directionally right but should be split so each sub-product carries its own 'replaces' line; as written, six-plus discrete products are invisible to anyone scanning the catalog by name.

Email scope risk: the catalog has 'Cloudflare Email Service' (outbound/transactional, correct) and 'DMARC Management' (report parsing, correct) but NO inbound threat-protection entry. Readers may wrongly assume Email Service or DMARC Management covers anti-phishing/BEC. They do not — that is the separate Cloudflare Email Security (Area 1) product. Add it and explicitly scope the three so they are not mistaken for one another.

'Cloudflare Email Service' replaces-line says inbound is handled by 'the separate inbound-parse webhook you'd wire up for replies.' Per /email-service/ docs, inbound is a first-class feature called Email Routing (route to custom addresses, Workers, or external destinations, with subaddressing/plus-addressing). Calling it just an 'inbound-parse webhook' undersells it; it is the direct replacement for ImprovMX / ForwardEmail / Google Workspace catch-all routing, not merely a SendGrid Inbound Parse equivalent.

Minor naming: 'Cloudflare Speed (Observatory)' — the live product index lists it as 'Speed' (Observatory is a feature within it). Not wrong, but the parenthetical pins it to an older sub-feature name. By contrast, 'Browser Run (formerly Browser Rendering)' IS current and correctly labeled — good catch there, no change needed.

No factual errors found in the storage/compute/AI core entries (Workers, R2, R2 SQL, KV, D1, Durable Objects, Queues, Workflows, Hyperdrive, Vectorize, Workers AI, AI Gateway, AI Search, Agents, Agent Memory, Containers, Sandbox SDK, Dynamic Workers, Workers for Platforms, Pipelines) — names and 'replaces' framings all match current docs. The catalog is strongest and fully trustworthy in the developer-platform core; the weakness is concentrated in the Zero Trust/SASE sub-products and the Rules group.
verdict: Strong catalog: ~95 entries with accurate, well-argued 'replaces' framing, and the developer-platform/storage/AI core (Workers, R2, D1, DOs, Queues, Workflows, Vectorize, Workers AI, Agents, etc.) is essentially complete and factually clean against current docs. The two glaring gaps are (1) the entire Rules / Ruleset Engine family (Transform Rules, Snippets, Cloud Connector, Redirect/Origin/Configuration/Compression Rules, Custom Errors) which is wholly absent, and (2) the Cloudflare One SASE sub-products — Access, Gateway, Browser Isolation, CASB, DLP, Email Security (Area 1), and DEX — which are collapsed into one umbrella line and therefore undiscoverable by name despite each having a clear standalone 'stop paying Okta/Zscaler/Netskope/Proofpoint' story. Close the Cloudflare One breakout first (highest 'replaces'-value per entry), then the Rules family.
